###### Article

# The creative sector accounts for 6,30% of total employment in Flanders, Belgium

Carlo Vuijlsteke, Flanders DC

23 April 2019

2 minutes read

The creative sector in Flanders, Belgium provides an important contribution to the economy and is a major source of employment and added value. The sector provides work to **171.265 full time equivalents** (6,30% of total employment), has a **turnover of € 78,8 billion** (13,39% of the total turnover) and a **gross value added of € 12,5 billion** (5,6% of the total gross value added in Flanders). Communications, pr and advertising, design and architecture showed the biggest increase in growth of employment and gross value added.

Share

![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a3bc10556/overview.png)![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a3bc10556/overview.png)
![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a3bc10556/overview.png)

Using the latest available data, the Flemish Department of Economy, Science and Innovation calculated the economic impact of the sector in a new study published in April 2019.

“The study shows that the sector has been growing year after year: between 2009 and the latest available data (2016) employment increased with 26% and gross value added with 42%”, says Pascal Cools, general manager of Flanders DC.

![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a479d7cc7/subsectors.png)
![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a479d7cc7/subsectors.png)

“The study was conducted on the basis of a series of NACE-codes (the EU classification system of economic activities) which were selected through desk research and a benchmark with studies in neighbouring countries”, says researcher Jan van Nispen of the Flemish Department of Economy, Science and Innovation.“We use the same top down-methodology for other sectors, which allows us to compare the creative sector with other industries. Still the results are an under-estimation, as there are many small enterprises in the creative sector whose economic activities are not captured in the databases.

Full time equivalents have increased most in communications, pr and advertising (+ 9.187 FTE’s), followed by design (+ 7.150 FTE’s) and architecture (+ 5.184 FTE’s). Digital and printed media is the only subsector with a decrease in FTE’s (- 1.591 FTE’s).

![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a4f2cc112/fte.png)
![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a4f2cc112/fte.png)

Full time equivalents

The same trend can be seen in gross value added. Gross added value increased most in design (+ € 1,08 billion), followed by communications, pr and advertising (+ € 785 million) and architecture (+ € 444 million). Digital and printed media is the only subsector with a decrease (- € 188 million).

![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a5143b94c/gross-value-added.png)
![](https://www.flandersdc.be/media/cache/image_small_png/uploads/media/5cd2a5143b94c/gross-value-added.png)

Gross value added

“These numbers and their growth over the years point to even more entrepreneurial potential. Other studies have also highlighted the role of the creative sector as a catalyst for innovation in other industries. The creative sector therefore deserves extra attention from policy makers” says Flemish minister of Work, Economy and Sports Philippe Muyters. “I see an important role for Flanders DC in developing its potential and its innovative spill-over effects to other sectors”.

Do you want to know more about it? Download [this pdf](/uploads/media/5cd2a97f635de/the-economic-impact-of-the-creative-sector.pdf?token=/uploads/media/5cd2a97f635de/the-economic-impact-of-the-creative-sector.pdf).

#### Creative Industries In Flanders Account For 7.2 Billion And Employ Over 120,000 People

A new report reveals that the creative and cultural sectors in Flanders account for 2.7% of GDP and together they represent a value of 7.2 billion euros. The creative industry employs 53 477 self-employed professionals (or 12.9 per cent of all self-employed workers in Flanders) and 69 983 employees.

This is the third impact study conducted by the Flanders DC Knowledge Centre at Antwerp Management School.

The following tables shows the main figures:

**Creative industries in Flanders (overall)**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **GDP FLanders** | **Self-employed** | **Employees** |
| **Creative Industries** | 7.2 billion | 53,477 | 69,983 |
| **Overall** | 265 billion | 415,592 | 2,911,767 |
| **Share of creative industries** | 2.7% | 12.9% | 2.4% |

**Text & image sector (books and press only)**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **Employees** | **Employers** | **Self-Employed** | **Added Value** | **Turnover** |
| **Print media (books)** | 6,702 (9.6%) | 521 (6.4%) | 2,964 (5.5%) | € 327,752,503 (4.6%) | € 1,007,294,178 (4.3%) |
| **Print media (press)** | 5,959 (8.5%) | 743 (9.1%) | 3,652 (6.8%) | € 685,105,166 (9.5%) | € 2,597,055,725 (11%) |
| **Overall creative industries** | 69,983 | 8,169 | 53,477 | 7.174.077.561 | € 23,562,680,031 |

Link to the [study (in Dutch](http://press.flandersdc.be/creatieve-industrie-in-vlaanderen-goed-voor--72-miljard))

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://www.cultuuronderzoek.be/wp-content/themes/cultuuronderzoek/assets/header-4.png)

# Culture and the creative economy in Flanders, Belgium

![](https://www.cultuuronderzoek.be/wp-content/themes/cultuuronderzoek/assets/knowledge-bank/knowledge-bank-3.png)

This report on Culture and the Creative Economy in Flanders, Belgium, has been compiled by the OECD with financial support from the Department of Culture, Youth, and Media of Flanders. The document includes findings from a case study review conducted as part of the OECD-EC project on Culture, Creative Industries, and Local Development, implemented by the OECD Local Employment and Economic Development (LEED) Programme and co-financed by the European Creative Europe Programme within the framework of the European Action Plan for Cultural Heritage.

The report highlights the strengths of the sector, identifies opportunities for policy measures, and shares innovative policy initiatives that can serve as inspiration for other regions looking to leverage cultural and creative sectors for local development.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Flanders boasts a thriving creative sector, contributing 6.3% to total employment and generating €78.8 billion in turnover across diverse industries like design, architecture, and advertising.
The creative industries in Flanders represent a value of €7.2 billion, accounting for 2.7% of GDP, highlighting significant growth in cultural exports and international collaborations.
The creative sector employs over 171,000 full-time equivalents, fostering job creation and innovation while supporting 12.9% of self-employed workers in the region.
